behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real git rename detection plus CLI parsing/output, which depends on subprocess interaction with git and real repository state. A mock parser fixture could miss rename-status nuances (e.g., Rxxx records) and hide real failures. No AI dependency, no multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI as a subprocess, and assert report shows 1 renamed and does not increment added/deleted for that rename.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Crash risk is tied to real git/subprocess output and binary diff handling at OS/process boundaries (encoding/stream behavior). Mocked text fixtures can always pass while missing subprocess/binary edge cases. No AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp git repo, add/modify a binary file, execute the CLI subprocess, and assert exit code is success and binary file changes are counted in the structured stdout report.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on real git behavior for unborn HEAD/no-commit repos (including exit codes/stderr), which is subprocess/OS-level interaction. Integration fakes can miss the exact failure mode. No AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a real git repo with zero commits, run the CLI subprocess, and assert successful execution with an empty structured report instead of an error.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: Correctness is defined against actual repository history semantics, requiring real git history creation and CLI subprocess execution. Pure module tests cannot validate end-to-end agreement with git’s real behavior. No AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo history containing added/deleted/renamed/modified files across commits, run the CLI subprocess, and assert reported category counts equal the known constructed history.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: This compares real git index vs working-tree semantics, which are repository-state and subprocess behaviors not faithfully captured by mocks. Needs real git staging operations and CLI invocation. No AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, make equivalent changes once unstaged and once staged, run CLI subprocess in both states, and assert identical structured stdout output.
